is not a valid l-value: A signal assigned inside an always block must be declared as `reg` (or `logic`), not `wire`. Change the declaration to `reg`/`logic`. Module outputs assigned procedurally must be `output reg`. Keep external inputs as `wire` and drive internal `reg` state from them.
not allowed in a constant expression: You referenced a `wire`/`reg` where Verilog requires a compile-time constant (parameter, localparam, part-select bounds, memory depth, replication count). Introduce a `localparam`/`parameter` for the constant, or move the sliced value into a separate signal and use that. Part-select indices and array sizes must be constants.
syntax error: Common causes: missing semicolon at end of a statement, unbalanced `begin`/`end`, using SystemVerilog constructs (logic, always_ff, typedef) while compiling with -g2012 not enabled, or an unterminated module. Re-check the line reported and the line before it. Prefer plain Verilog-2005 constructs unless SystemVerilog is required.
port .* is not a port of: The instantiated module was connected with a port name that does not exist on that module. Make instantiation port names exactly match the submodule declaration, or use ordered connection. Verify the submodule definition is included in the compile set.
Unknown module type: iverilog could not find the definition of an instantiated module. Ensure every instantiated submodule's source file is passed to iverilog, or define the module. Do not instantiate modules that are not part of the generated source set.
already declared: A net/variable/module name is declared twice in the same scope. Remove the duplicate declaration or rename one. Check for a port declared both in the header and again in the body.
reset is not a valid l-value in riscv_pi: `reset` was declared as a `wire`/`input`. In a testbench the stimulus signals driven inside `initial`/`always` blocks must be `reg`. Declare `reg reset;` and drive it; keep DUT inputs connected to these regs.
A reference to a wire or reg (`addr['sd1: A part-select of a wire/reg was used in a constant context (parameter/initial). Move the slice into its own `wire [10:0] addr_slice = addr[10:0];` and use a genuine constant (localparam) where a constant is required.
A reference to a wire or reg (`addr_slic: A `wire` cannot be used in a constant expression. Use a `localparam` for true constants, or a `reg` where a procedurally-computed value is needed. Constant contexts require compile-time values.
yosys: unexpected '[' arrayed port: Yosys/OpenLane cannot synthesize arrayed (unpacked array) module ports. Flatten arrayed ports to packed vectors, e.g. replace `input [7:0] data [0:3]` with `input [31:0] data_flat` and slice internally.
multiple driver: A net is driven by more than one source (two `assign`s or an `assign` plus an `always`). Ensure each net has a single driver; combine the logic into one always block or one continuous assignment.
implicit definition of wire: A signal was used before being declared. Declare all nets/regs explicitly. Add `default_nettype none` at the top to catch typos early, then declare every signal.
sensitivity list: For combinational logic use `always @(*)` (or `always_comb`) so all read signals are in the sensitivity list; for sequential logic use `always @(posedge clk ...)`. Incomplete sensitivity lists cause simulation/synthesis mismatches.
